mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate compresses the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an approaching recession, which compresses forward earnings expectations and triggers equity risk-off positioning, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in consumer credit extensions and residential construction activity indicates robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive signal fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns in >50% of out-of-sample instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production capacity outpaces demand constraints, reducing input cost inflation and supporting near-term equity risk premiums, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5-day forward returns in >60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains elevated but policy rates are near zero, real interest rates turn deeply negative, forcing institutional portfolio rebalancing into equities and driving positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Real rate compression fails to generate positive SPX returns when trigger conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding historical trend implies an expanding corporate earnings base, which mechanically translates to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth and 60-day forward SPX returns show zero correlation over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and a rising unemployment rate indicates labor market inefficiency and wage-push inflation fears, triggering short-term equity risk-off positioning and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Payroll-unemployment divergence does not predict negative 5-day forward returns in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts collapsing below 1.3 million units signals a sharp demand shock to construction and related sectors, dragging down aggregate economic activity and producing negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Housing starts below threshold consistently coincide with positive 20-day forward returns.
